description: Storing passwords with MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256 or an unsalted fast hash, making offline cracking trivial after a breach.
body: Failure mode: password verifiers are computed with a fast, general-purpose hash and possibly no salt, so after a database leak an attacker brute-forces or uses rainbow tables to recover passwords at enormous speed. Detection signals: password column stores MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256 output; identical passwords yield identical hashes (no per-user salt); no work-factor parameters stored. Do not use a fast or unsalted hash for passwords. Safer fallback: rehash with a memory-hard salted KDF (Argon2id, scrypt, or bcrypt) at current parameters, store algorithm and parameters for upgrades, and migrate existing users by rehashing on next successful login.
